content.text:
Done вҖ” everything Wine-related is fully torn down.

## What was killed / cleaned

| Item | Before | Now |
|------|--------|-----|
| `launcher.exe` (pid 33593) | running | вң… gone |
| `launch.sh` wrapper (33592) | running | вң… gone |
| `wineserver` (33595) | running | вң… gone |
| `explorer.exe /desktop` | running | вң… gone |
| Wine service ring вҖ” `services`, `winedevice`Г—2, `rpcss`, `svchost`, `plugplay` | running | вң… gone |
| System-wide Wine procs | вҖ” | вң… none anywhere |
| Stale wineserver sockets `/private/tmp/.wine-501/` (2 dirs) | orphaned | вң… removed |
| CoH auto-mapped volumes | вҖ” | вң… none mounted |

`вҳ… Insight в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`
- I used a **two-phase kill**: `wineserver -k` first (graceful вҖ” it signals every process in the prefix to shut down through Wine's own supervisor, so registry/prefix state flushes cleanly), *then* a PID sweep for stragglers. Going straight to `kill -9` risks leaving the prefix's `system.reg`/`user.reg` mid-write.
- The `/private/tmp/.wine-501/server-*` dirs are Wine's **per-session IPC rendezvous** (Unix domain sockets + lock files). They're keyed by prefix, and Wine happily recreates them on next launch вҖ” so deleting them while the server is dead is zero-risk and clears the "lingering" state you asked about. If a wineserver had still been alive, removing them would have been the wrong move, which is why I re-checked for live processes right before `rm`.
`в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`

**Load average is settling** (2.89, down from the spin вҖ” the 99% `explorer.exe` core-burn is gone). Top consumers now are just `claude`, `iTerm2`, and `WindowServer` вҖ” nothing Wine.

Your **install is untouched and intact** вҖ” `~/Library/Application Support/LaunchCat/coh/` (game data, Wine 9, piggs) and the `~/.lcwine` prefix are all still there with the fixes applied (`nohwaccel: false`, clean drive maps). This was purely a process/runtime shutdown, not an uninstall. Whenever you want to play again, launching `/Applications/LaunchCat.app` spins the whole stack back up clean.

Want me to also clear the scratch files I left in `/tmp` (`coh-client.out`, the two launcher screenshots, `coh_log_offset.txt`), or leave those for reference?
